description: Length-matched V2 facts for quantum mechanics domain.

facts.truth: position and momentum cannot both be precisely known simultaneously
distractors: uncertainty arises solely from imprecision of measurement apparatus
energy is fundamentally uncertain regardless of time interval used
the principle applies only to massless photons not massive matter

facts.truth: upon measurement, yielding one definite outcome from superposition
distractors: during unitary time evolution governed by Schrodinger equation
from particle-particle interactions without any external observation
spontaneously without any external triggering process or interaction

facts.context: The Pauli exclusion principle applies to:
facts.truth: all fermions, which cannot occupy the same quantum state simultaneously
distractors: all bosons, which are forbidden from sharing identical quantum states
every particle regardless of its spin statistics or quantum numbers
only electrons in atomic orbitals, not other fermionic particle types

facts.truth: penetrate through classically forbidden energy barriers with nonzero probability
distractors: exceed the speed of light by borrowing energy from the vacuum temporarily
spontaneously gain energy from quantum vacuum fluctuation processes nearby
violate conservation of energy by passing through any arbitrary potential

facts.context: Spin-1/2 particles return to their original state after:
facts.truth: 720 degree (4-pi) rotation, requiring two full turns for identity
distractors: 360 degree (2-pi) rotation, identical to classical rigid body objects
180 degree (pi) rotation, as expected from the half-integer spin value
any arbitrary rotation angle, since spin state is rotationally invariant

facts.context: The Born rule states that measurement probability equals:
facts.truth: the squared modulus of the wave function amplitude at that point
distractors: the wave function value itself without taking the modulus squared
the energy eigenvalue of the quantum state being measured directly
the expectation value of the position operator for that given state

facts.context: Degenerate energy levels are split by:
facts.truth: perturbations that reduce the system symmetry, lifting the degeneracy
distractors: uniformly increasing the particle mass without changing the potential
cooling the system toward absolute zero temperature and ground state
adding more identical particles into the same quantum energy level

facts.truth: violate Bell inequalities, ruling out all local hidden variable models
distractors: can be fully explained by classical shared information at preparation
enable faster-than-light communication of usable information signals
require the entangled particles to remain in physical contact always

facts.truth: exists in a linear combination of multiple states until measurement occurs
distractors: rapidly switches between definite states too fast for detection methods
occupies one definite state that is simply unknown to the observer here
simultaneously exists in all possible states with exactly equal weights

facts.context: Quantum harmonic oscillator ground state energy is:
facts.truth: nonzero at half hbar omega, the irreducible zero-point energy
distractors: exactly zero at the lowest energy state as in classical mechanics
negative due to the attractive nature of the restoring potential well
undefined because position uncertainty prevents energy determination

facts.context: Hydrogen atom energy levels depend primarily on:
facts.truth: principal quantum number n with E proportional to negative 1/n-squared
distractors: orbital angular momentum quantum number l as the dominant parameter
spin quantum number s which determines splitting of all energy levels
magnetic quantum number m which sets the energy in zero external field

facts.truth: incompatible measurements connected by a fundamental uncertainty relation
distractors: the two observables can still be measured simultaneously with precision
one observable must always vanish whenever the other one is measured
the mathematical operators are undefined when applied in any sequence
